entries.question: How should I prepare for end-semester exams?
entries.answer: Start at least 4 weeks early. Work through previous-year papers - patterns repeat. Form a small study group and use the library reading hall during peak prep weeks.

entries.question: What advice do seniors give first-year students?
entries.answer: Attend orientation, join one club early to build friends, don't skip the first month of classes, and find your class CR - they will be your lifeline for notices and notes.

entries.question: How do I clear a backlog subject?
entries.answer: Register for the supplementary exam within the window announced by the Exam Cell (usually 2 weeks after results). Pay Rs.600 per subject and request notes from the faculty early.

entries.question: When and how should I look for internships?
entries.answer: Start applying by the end of 4th semester for summer slots. Use the Placement Cell, LinkedIn, and Internshala. Build a basic resume and have a senior or faculty mentor review it.
